name: Photos Picker
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Photos Picker (PhotosUI)

IMPORT & USAGE:
- import PhotosUI
- @State var selectedItem: PhotosPickerItem?
- @State var selectedImage: UIImage?
- PhotosPicker(selection: $selectedItem, matching: .images) { Label("Choose Photo", systemImage: "photo") }
- Load in .onChange(of: selectedItem) — see canonical code.

LOADING IMAGE FROM PhotosPickerItem:
- Use item.loadTransferable(type: Data.self) — async/await pattern.
- Convert Data to UIImage on success.
- Handle loading failure gracefully with an error state.

MULTI-SELECT:
- @State var selectedItems: [PhotosPickerItem] = []
- PhotosPicker(selection: $selectedItems, maxSelectionCount: 5, matching: .images)
- Process each item in a loop with loadTransferable.

VIDEO SELECTION:
- matching: .videos for videos only.
- matching: .any(of: [.images, .videos]) for both.

KEY POINT: PhotosPicker does NOT require any special permission — it uses a privacy-preserving out-of-process picker. Do NOT request Photos library access (PHPhotoLibrary) unless you need full album management.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT use UIImagePickerController — PhotosPicker is the modern SwiftUI replacement.
- Do NOT use PHPickerViewController with UIViewControllerRepresentable — use the native SwiftUI PhotosPicker.
- Do NOT request PHPhotoLibrary authorization just to pick photos — PhotosPicker handles privacy automatically.
- Do NOT assume loadTransferable always succeeds — handle nil/error.
antiPatterns: Requesting PHPhotoLibrary authorization for simple photo picking (PhotosPicker does not need it)
Not handling loadTransferable failure (returns nil or throws)
